INSTRUCTIONS:
Operation Span Task

In this task, you will try to memorize letters you see on the screen while you also solve simple math problems.

In the next few minutes you will have a practice task that will allow you to see how this task works.

First, we are going to practice the letter memory task.

Click on NEXT to begin.
LETTERS:
Memory Practice

For this practice task, letters will appear on the screen one at a time. Try to remember each letter in the order they are presented.

After you see 2-3 letters presented, you will see a screen with 12 possible letters.

Your task: Select each letter in the order presented using your mouse.

Click on NEXT.
LETTERS2:
Memory Practice

When you have selected all the letters, and they are in the correct order, hit the DONE button.

  • If you make a mistake, hit the CLEAR button to start over.
  • If you forget one of the letters, click the BLANK button to mark its position.

    Remember: It is very important to get the letters in the same order as you see them. If you forget one, use the BLANK button to mark the position.

    Click on NEXT to begin.
NUMBERS:
Math Practice

Now you will practice the math part of this task.

A math problem will appear on your screen, like this:

6 - 2 + 3 = ?


As soon as you see the math problem, compute the correct answer. In the above problem, the answer 7 is correct.

When you know the correct answer, click the mouse button.

Click NEXT to continue.
NUMBERS2:
Math Practice

On the next screen, you will see a number displayed along with buttons marked TRUE and FALSE.

  • If the number is the correct answer to the math problem, click TRUE.
  • If it is not the correct answer, click FALSE.

    For example, if you see the problem:

    6 - 2 + 3 = ?


    And the number on the following screen is 7, click TRUE (correct answer).

    If the number shown is 6, click FALSE (incorrect - the answer is 7, not 6).

    Click NEXT to continue.
NUMBERS3:
Math Practice

It is VERY important that you:

  • Get the math problems correct
  • Solve the problems as quickly as you can


    Eventually this task will be interleaved with the memory task, so during practice you will see a series of problems (for now, just 1 or 2) before you get feedback on them all. Later, it will match the length of the list of letters you will be remembering. Click NEXT when you are ready to practice some math problems.
PRACBATTERY1:
Memory + Math Practice

Now you will practice doing both parts of the task at the same time.

In the next practice set, you will be given a math problem. Once you make your decision about the problem, a letter will appear on the screen. Try and remember the letter.

In the previous section where you only did the math problems, the computer computed your average time to solve the problems. If you take longer than your average time, the computer will automatically move you onto the next letter part, thus skipping the True or False part and will count that problem as a math error.

Therefore it is VERY important to solve the problems as quickly and as accurately as possible.

Click NEXT.
PRACBATTERY2:
Memory + Math Practice

After the letter goes away, another math problem will appear, and then another letter.

At the end of each set of letters and math problems, a recall screen will appear. Use the mouse to select the letters you just saw. Try your best to get the letters in the correct order.

Important:
  • Work QUICKLY and ACCURATELY on the math problems
  • Make sure you know the answer before clicking to the next screen
  • You will not be told if your answer is correct

    After the recall screen, you will be given feedback about your performance on both the number of letters recalled and the percent correct on the math problems.

    Click NEXT to continue.
PRACBATTERY3:
Memory + Math Practice

During the feedback, you will see a number in red in the top right of the screen. This indicates your percent correct for the math problems for the entire experiment.

It is VERY important for you to keep this at least at 85%. For our purposes, we can only use data where the participant was at least 85% accurate on the math problems.

Therefore, in order for you to be asked to come back for future experiments, you must perform at least at 85% on the math problems WHILE doing your best to recall as many letters as possible.
Click NEXT to try some practice problems.
TRIALS:
Begin Main Task

That is the end of the practice.

The real task will look like the practice trials you just completed. First you will get a math problem to solve, then a letter to remember.

When you see the recall screen, select the letters in the order presented. If you forget a letter, click the BLANK box to mark where it should go.

Some of the sets will have more math problems and letters than others.

Remember:
  • Work as QUICKLY and ACCURATELY as possible on math problems
  • Keep your math problem accuracy at 85% or above
  • Do your best to recall as many letters as possible

    Click NEXT to begin the task.
SECONDCHANCE:
Practice Again

You did not meet the required math accuracy criterion of 85% on that practice round.

It is very important that you maintain at least 85% accuracy on the math problems while also remembering as many letters as possible.

You will now have one more chance to practice the combined task. Please focus on being both quick and accurate on the math problems.

Click NEXT to try the practice again.
PROCEED_DESPITE_THRESHOLD:
Proceeding to Main Task

You did not meet the required math accuracy criterion of 85% during practice.

However, you will now proceed to the main task. Please remember:

For your data to be used, you must reach at least 85% accuracy on the math problems during the test.

Focus on being both quick and accurate on the math problems while doing your best to recall as many letters as possible.

Click NEXT to begin the main task.
FEEDBACK_DT: You got %CORRECT_D%/%NUM_D% problems correct and %CORRECT_T%/%NUM_T% target letters correct
CLICK_PROMPT: Click mouse when you solve the problem
